parts.title: Social Studies Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a grade [X] class in [Social Studies subject, e.g. Civics] that meets the curriculum goals on [specific focus, e.g. democratic processes], with an introduction and three activities.

Write a short guide for teaching a grade [X] class to analyse a [specific source, e.g. historical text] in [Social Studies subject]. Also provide a concrete example that students can understand and use as a template.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. sustainable development] into a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, with a discussion for each.
Create a list of five short, encouraging phrases to build a grade [X] student's confidence during a Social Studies discussion or task.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] within [area] with a short paired conversation exercise for students.
Suggest three ways to use everyday examples (e.g. [specific example, e.g. news stories]) in a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, with one activity for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute closing routine for a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, covering three elements (e.g. reflection, summary).

Give me five ideas for making a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] more memorable for a grade [X] class, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. historical events], with one activity for each idea.
Create a list of three short reflection questions for a grade [X] class after a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] on [specific theme, e.g. human rights], to capture their thoughts.

Suggest three ways to collaborate with English teachers at a [primary/lower secondary school] to connect [Social Studies subject] to [specific theme, e.g. global perspectives], with one idea for each approach.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a grade [X] class's understanding of [subject area, e.g. European map geography], with three criteria.
Give me five strategies for motivating a grade [X] student who finds Social Studies boring, with one engaging idea for each.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. websites, films) for a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. source criticism].
Suggest three ways to adapt a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] student with [specific need, e.g. language difficulties], with one solution for each.
Create a template for a weekly plan for [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. facts, analysis, discussion).
Give me five ideas for using minimal resources in a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class to explore [specific theme, e.g. migration], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a grade [X] class to [specific concept, e.g. democracy] in [Social Studies subject], with one discussion task for each.
Write a short guide for managing a lively discussion in a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, with three steps and one de-escalation strategy.

Give me an example of a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class that combines the subject with [specific theme, e.g. the impact of technology], with three concrete elements.
Create five short exit ticket questions I can use at the end of a lesson on [subject area] in [Social Studies subject] in grade [X] to capture what students have and have not understood.
Create a lesson for a grade [X] class in [Social Studies subject] where they analyse a [specific issue, e.g. climate policy], with three steps and a concrete learning goal.
Give me five ways to make a History lesson more engaging for a grade [X] class, with one creative idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a grade [X] class to compare [specific topic, e.g. religions] in [Social Studies subject], with three steps and one example.
Suggest three ways to connect a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class to [specific theme, e.g. globalisation], with one activity for each.
Create a full-year plan for a grade [X] class in [Social Studies subject] at a school in Sweden. Students have [Y] hours per week over [Z weeks] and each lesson is [XX minutes] long.
Give me an example of a routine for closing a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class with a short debate, with three steps.
Suggest ten ways to use digital tools (e.g. [specific tool, e.g. Google Earth]) in a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, with one task for each.

Give me five ideas for integrating role play into a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. historical perspectives], with one role play scenario for each.
Write a short template for a 5-minute reflection after a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, with three questions about their learning.
Create a list of three short ways to support a grade [X] student who is struggling with [specific concept, e.g. power structures] in [Social Studies subject], with one example for each.

Suggest five ways to collaborate with Art teachers at a [lower secondary school] to connect [Social Studies subject] to [specific theme, e.g. visual historical storytelling], with one concrete idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a grade [X] class to analyse a news article in [Social Studies subject] during one lesson. The plan should include three steps.
Write a list of five simple texts or films for a grade [X] class in [Social Studies subject] that reinforce [specific goal, e.g. geographical understanding], with a rationale for each.
Suggest five ways to handle a grade [X] class that is reluctant to participate in discussion during a Social Studies lesson in [Social Studies subject], with one concrete method for each.
Create a template for a term plan for [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. facts, analysis, social issues) and time frames.
Give me five ideas for creating a simple presentation of a Social Studies project in [Social Studies subject] with a grade [X] class, with minimal preparation, and one element for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a grade [X] class to [specific social concept, e.g. justice] in [Social Studies subject], with one activity for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a grade [X] student with [specific need, e.g. concentration difficulties] during a lesson in [Social Studies subject], with three steps.
Give me an example of a lesson in [Social Studies subject] for a grade [X] class that combines Social Studies with [specific theme, e.g. digitalisation], with three elements.

Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the student council at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. understanding of democracy] in [Social Studies subject], with two examples for each approach.
